channel.title: Comments for page "Former Assets"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Former Assets"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know that at this point it is essentially necroposting, but damn. I loved this story, an incredible mixture of the truly bizarre, the outright horrific, and the charm of a little girl lost.

Well written, well written indeed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was the first successful tale of this Canon. Every (standalone) tale set before this one is a prequel and grouped together under Opening Acts. There's actually about a twenty-year gap between this tale and overthrowing Fuller, which won't be mentioned for several more tales.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That was a straight up enjoyable read :)

But… when did Motormouth get captured by the Foundation? Wasn't he part of the mutiny only a couple chapters back? Did he go off-campus sometime in between and run afoul of an MTF?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Pink Elephant got into the grownup juice and now it’s having hallucinations about people.

I fucking love this Canon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
oh wow, this tale is fantastic.

im definitely reading more of this tale series later!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love it. Love everything about it. The bit about the Ringmaster being the leader of the clowns was a nice bit of information.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To be fair, they do travel to so many alternate universes and other countries that it makes sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ohgod the elephant pun…

It was a fun read, getting paid in 'scrip' is a thing I've heard before…

'One scrip, two scrip. Red scrip, blue scrip…' I've learned to be rather weary of any employer who pays their employees in currency that's only redeemable in their own company.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An enjoyable look into the circus. I noticed instances of repetition and the dialogue seemed a bit perfunctory when it was setting up in the beginning, but the portrayal of the bizarre circus is fun, it might be a little rough around the edges but it does succeed in showing the reader an insight into an oddball setting. I wish more had actually happened other than the world building and characterization, even if you plan on following this up it's kind of irksome that nothing really happens, there's lot of setup and not a lot of payoff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Top tier and I hope theirs a sequel planned cause I'd love to read about Victor's relecutant day off spent at this circus. I can imagine so much. The balloon the screams when you pop it, that was great. And I loved the drunken elephant that's works. ….Wait for it. Works for peanuts.. lol. This was great.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Much better than I last read it.
